opp.name: Sunrun S2P
opp.next_step: 6/23mc- Reached out to Tim Crane to introduce myself as the new Zycus point of contact taking over from Kevin Cipollaro. Communication was positioned to avoid restarting the conversation and confirmed that we are aligned to Sunrun’s previously discussed priorities around PR-to-PO automation, invoice matching, compliance controls, AI, Oracle Cloud, and Google Workspace. Sent a 45-minute meeting placeholder for this thursday june 25th. Next step is to secure the meeting, include a Solutions Consultant, validate Sunrun’s 2026 procurement priorities, and determine the right path for a more practical solution discussion focused on Oracle Cloud integration, Merlin Intake, and measurable business value.
opp.ais_why:
• Customer states a strategic goal to leverage AI in 2026, with a recent mandate to freeze Supply Chain headcount and reduce costs. Explicit AI mandate.
• Urgency-driven customer voice: "don't spend the time doing our due diligence now we'll be left behind."
• Plan is to re-engage and leverage ANA insights, and the description flags exploration of AI-driven automation for the supplier interface. ANA explicitly planned.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-12-12, Tim Crane
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23, Rick Taranek outreach confirming priorities
metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated operational pain around PR-to-PO automation, invoice matching, compliance controls, Oracle Cloud integration, and Google Workspace integration on 2025-12-12 discovery call. Tim Crane stated 'trying to figure out what we can do to help optimize our whole procurement process' and named specific systems (Oracle Procurement Cloud, Google Workspace). However, no quantified impact (cost savings, cycle time reduction, headcount efficiency, error rate) was captured. The pain is real but unanchored to a business case. Without a quantified value proposition the deal lacks the urgency and executive sponsorship required to move from investigation mode to a funded project. Next move: build a quantified ROI model anchored to Sunrun's procurement spend and cycle-time baseline.

sources: Avoma 2025-12-12, Tim Crane
SF OpportunityContactRole: Tim Crane, Director
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23: Rick outreach, no buyer reply confirmed
196-day stall with no champion-led activity
champion.narrative: Tim Crane (Director, Strategic Sourcing) is the sole stakeholder, but he shows weak champion characteristics. On 2025-12-12 he was curious and exploratory ('investigation mode', 'still investigating', 'too early for RFP'), but he did not drive urgency, introduce Zycus to the SVP or peers, or push the mid-January 2026 multi-stakeholder session he mentioned. The 196-day silence and zero follow-through signal passive interest, not active advocacy. Rick Taranek reached out 2026-06-23 to reboot the relationship after an owner handoff, but buyer reply is unconfirmed. Tim has not demonstrated access to the Economic Buyer, ability to influence the decision, or willingness to champion Zycus against Oracle or inertia. He is a contact, not a champion. Next move: test Tim's commitment on the Rick meeting; if he re-engages, ask him to introduce the SVP and co-sponsor a procurement transformation workshop. If he does not reply, escalate to the SVP directly via executive connect.

sources: Avoma 2025-12-12, Tim Crane: 'we use Oracle Procurement'
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23: Rick confirmed 'Oracle Cloud' integration priority
196-day stall: inertia / do-nothing is the real competitor
competition.narrative: Sunrun is an Oracle Procurement Cloud shop (confirmed 2025-12-12 call, Tim Crane stated 'we use Oracle Procurement' and are exploring 'what we can do to help optimize'). The primary competitor is Oracle Procurement Cloud as the incumbent. Tim's language ('still investigating', 'too early for RFP') suggests Sunrun may expand Oracle capabilities, integrate a complementary tool, or replace Oracle with a broader S2P platform. The competitive wedge is Merlin AI/automation (Intake, ANA, Agentic AI) layered on Oracle integration, positioning Zycus as the intelligence layer that makes Oracle work better rather than a rip-and-replace. However, the 196-day stall and absence of urgency suggest 'do-nothing / delay' is the stronger competitor than Oracle itself. How we win: demonstrate quantified ROI on PR-to-PO automation and invoice matching, anchor Merlin Intake as the AI differentiator Oracle cannot match, and secure SVP sponsorship to overcome inertia.

sources: Avoma 2025-12-12, Tim Crane: 'trying to figure out what we can do to help optimize our whole procurement process'
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23: Rick confirmed alignment to 'PR-to-PO automation, invoice matching, compliance controls, AI, Oracle Cloud, Google Workspace'
identify_pain.narrative: Tim Crane articulated clear operational pain on 2025-12-12: Sunrun is running Oracle Procurement Cloud and Google Workspace and struggling with PR-to-PO automation, invoice matching, and compliance controls. He stated 'trying to figure out what we can do to help optimize our whole procurement process' and expressed interest in AI/automation (Merlin Intake) to reduce manual work. The pain is operational (process inefficiency, manual touchpoints) and tied to their Oracle and Google stack. However, no quantified impact (cost, headcount, error rate) was captured, and no executive pain owner (CFO, SVP Procurement) has validated the urgency. The pain is real but unsponsored. Next move: anchor the pain to a quantified business case and elevate it to an executive who owns the P&L impact.

sources: No contracting or legal discussion in Avoma 2025-12-12
No legal/procurement contacts in SF OpportunityContactRole or tasks
paper_process.narrative: No paper process visibility. No discussion of MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement occurred on the 2025-12-12 call or in subsequent Salesforce activity. Sunrun is a publicly traded company; enterprise contracting with legal/InfoSec/procurement gates is certain, but no stakeholder from those functions has engaged. The 196-day stall and absence of contracting discovery suggests the buyer has not reached the stage where paper process matters. Next move: when (if) the deal re-engages, map the procurement/legal/InfoSec owners and typical cycle time for a $500k SaaS+services contract at Sunrun.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Tim Crane (Director) only
Avoma 2025-12-12: Tim mentioned SVP and director-level peers but no follow-up
SF Tasks/Events: no EB-level activity
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged after 196 days. Tim Crane (Director, Strategic Sourcing) is the sole contact; he stated on 2025-12-12 that he would loop in 'SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain' and 'directors of planning, procurement, and logistics' for a mid-January 2026 session, but that meeting never materialized. No C-level or budget-holder sponsor has appeared in contact roles, tasks, or calls. For a $500k source-to-pay platform deal at a publicly traded company the absence of an Economic Buyer is fatal. The evidence shows Tim is in discovery mode, not empowered to drive commercial decisions. Next move: demand an executive connect with the SVP or CFO to validate budget, authority, and timeline before investing further in use-case work.

sources: Avoma 2025-12-12, Tim Crane
SF contact roles and tasks: single-threaded, no cross-functional activity
decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. Tim Crane stated on 2025-12-12 it was 'too early to say we're gonna have an RFP' and he was 'still investigating'. He mentioned convening directors of planning, procurement, and logistics plus the SVP for a mid-January 2026 follow-up, but that session never occurred. No approval chain documented (Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c fields unavailable). The call sequence shows only one discovery call 196 days ago; no escalation, no multi-stakeholder engagement, no procurement/legal/InfoSec joining. The absence of process clarity after 196 days signals the buyer is not running an active evaluation. Next move: on the Rick Taranek meeting (if confirmed), pin down the approval chain, timeline, and whether Sunrun will run a competitive RFP or sole-source Zycus.

sources: Avoma 2025-12-12, Tim Crane
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23
decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer outlined high-level evaluation priorities on 2025-12-12: PR-to-PO automation, invoice matching, compliance controls, Oracle Cloud integration (they are an Oracle Procurement Cloud shop), Google Workspace integration, and AI/automation capabilities (Merlin Intake). Tim Crane stated they are 'trying to figure out what we can do to help optimize' and are 'still investigating' options. However, no formal RFP/RFI, no weighted scorecard, no test-case validation, and no structured use-case sessions occurred in 196 days. The criteria are directional but not codified. Rick Taranek's 2026-06-23 outreach confirmed alignment to these priorities, but buyer has not committed to a formal evaluation process. Next move: propose a half-day workshop to translate these priorities into a formal decision framework and expose competing alternatives.

Rep reached out 2026-06-23 (owner handoff from Kevin Cipollaro to Rick Taranek, meeting placeholder sent for 2026-06-25) but buyer reply unconfirmed. Typical enterprise motion from Qualified to close is 12-15 months; this deal has 154 days and no EB/DM mapped. At this pace deal will not hit 2026-11-27 without aggressive executive intervention.

flags: Single-threaded relationship: only Tim Crane (Director, Strategic Sourcing) is engaged after 196 days. The promised multi-stakeholder meeting has not occurred. Map the full buying committee (directors of planning, procurement, logistics per Tim's description, plus the Economic Buyer—likely the SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain) and request introductions in the next confirmed meeting.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged: no C-level or budget-holder sponsor identified or involved after 196 days on a $500k platform deal at a publicly traded company. Explicitly ask Tim: 'Who controls the budget and final sign-off for an investment of this scale?' and request an executive stakeholder session to validate the business case and timeline.

Ownership handoff re-engagement risk: Mario Castro reached out 2026-06-23 (3 days ago) after 196-day silence, but buyer confirmation for the 2026-06-25 meeting is unverified. If Tim does not respond within 5 business days, escalate to a direct phone call (not email) and propose a brief 15-minute re-alignment call to confirm current priorities and next steps before attempting a full stakeholder session.

deltas.subject: Owner handoff mid-cycle: Kevin Cipollaro to Rick Taranek handoff after 196-day silence adds relationship risk; Rick's 2026-06-23 outreach awaiting buyer reply.

Ownership handoff from Kevin Cipollaro to Mario Castro after 196-day silence. Mario reached out 2026-06-23 to reintroduce himself and sent a meeting placeholder for 2026-06-25, but buyer confirmation is unverified. Risk: relationship reset may delay re-engagement or require re-education of new owner on prior discovery.

Single-threaded relationship: only Tim Crane (Director, Strategic Sourcing) is engaged after 196 days. The promised multi-stakeholder meeting has not occurred. Map the full buying committee (directors of planning, procurement, logistics per Tim's description, plus the Economic Buyer—likely the SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain) and request introductions in the next confirmed meeting.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged: no C-level or budget-holder sponsor identified or involved after 196 days on a $500k platform deal at a publicly traded company. Explicitly ask Tim: 'Who controls the budget and final sign-off for an investment of this scale?' and request an executive stakeholder session to validate the business case and timeline.

Ownership handoff re-engagement risk: Mario Castro reached out 2026-06-23 (3 days ago) after 196-day silence, but buyer confirmation for the 2026-06-25 meeting is unverified. If Tim does not respond within 5 business days, escalate to a direct phone call (not email) and propose a brief 15-minute re-alignment call to confirm current priorities and next steps before attempting a full stakeholder session.

value.name: Tim Crane
value.risk: Weak champion: no EB access, no advocacy demonstrated, no urgency. May not have authority or political capital to drive $500k platform decision.
value.title: Director, Strategic Sourcing
value.sentiment: Curious but passive; 'investigation mode', no follow-through on mid-January 2026 multi-stakeholder session, 196-day silence.

value.name: Tim Crane
value.risk: Champion power unconfirmed. Did not drive follow-up meeting or stakeholder expansion in 196 days, suggesting either lack of executive mandate or competing priorities. Ownership handoff to Mario Castro may have disrupted relationship continuity.
value.title: Director, Strategic Sourcing
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma discovery call 2025-12-12; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-23
value.sentiment: Positive on 2025-12-12 discovery call; proactive, collaborative, articulated quantified pain. Current sentiment unknown after 196-day silence and unconfirmed 2026-06-25 meeting.

packets.subject: Tim Crane

value.name: the SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain
value.risk: Not identified or engaged after 196 days; critical gap for $500k platform deal.
value.title: SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain
value.sentiment: Unknown; mentioned by Tim Crane 2025-12-12 but never engaged.

value.name: the SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain
value.risk: Not identified on contact roles, not on any call, not referenced by Tim Crane. Likely budget holder for a $500k source-to-pay platform investment. Must be mapped and engaged before any forecast upgrade.
value.title: SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain
value.source: SF Account contacts (title inferred from account-level contact list, but no opp contact role or engagement evidence)

packets.subject: the SVP, Global Procurement & Supply Chain

value.name: Oracle Procurement Cloud
value.how_we_win: Merlin AI (Intake, Agentic negotiation, ANA analytics) as intelligence layer on Oracle; quantified ROI on PR-to-PO automation and invoice matching that Oracle alone cannot deliver. Make it additive, not rip-and-replace.

value.name: Oracle Procurement Cloud
value.quote: We went live on that at the beginning of this past year... we are not leveraging technology as much as I think we could be on the whole source to contract and procure to pay activity.
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the innovation layer on top of Oracle—fill the source-to-contract gap, enhance P2P with agentic AI, and protect the Oracle investment rather than rip-and-replace.

packets.subject: Oracle Procurement Cloud

value.name: Do-nothing / delay
value.quote: still investigating, too early to say we're gonna have an RFP
value.how_we_win: Anchor quantified pain (cost, cycle time, compliance risk) to SVP/CFO; create urgency via executive sponsor and tie to a forcing event (budget cycle, Oracle renewal, compliance deadline).

value.name: Do-nothing / delay
value.quote: I think it's probably too early for us to say we're gonna have an RFP... we're in the investigation mode right now. I can't tell you if it's first quarter or third quarter.
value.how_we_win: Convert investigation to action by co-building a phased 2026 roadmap with ROI milestones, positioning the Q3/Q4 2026 window as the last chance to hit 2027 cost-reduction targets before the starting gun is missed (buyer's own language).

packets.subject: Do-nothing / delay

gaps: Economic Buyer / budget authority not identified or engaged after 196 days (knowledge gap)
Multi-stakeholder engagement: promised mid-January 2026 session did not occur, single-threaded with Tim Crane (knowledge gap)
Quantified business case / Metrics: no cost savings, cycle time, headcount efficiency, or ROI quantified (knowledge gap)
Decision process and timeline: buyer stated 'too early for RFP', no approval chain documented (knowledge gap)
Paper process (legal, InfoSec, procurement): no contracting or legal discussion, no stakeholders from these functions engaged (knowledge gap)
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Omitted. Use')
